description_long: This is a large, circular stone castle with high crenellated walls and four prominent round towers, each topped with conical gray roofs and golden flags. The main entrance is an arched gateway flanked by two smaller towers, and decorative banners hang on either side of the entrance. The castle walls are punctuated by evenly spaced battlements and small arrow slits, providing defensive features typical of medieval fortifications. The central courtyard is open to the sky, surrounded by the inner face of the walls and the bases of the towers. The tallest tower rises above the others, likely serving as a keep or lookout. The overall structure is robust and imposing, with a gray stone appearance and minimal ornamentation beyond the banners and flags.
description: A circular gray stone castle with four round towers, battlements, and golden flags, featuring a prominent arched entrance and decorative banners.
description_view_0: Front view showing the main arched entrance, two flanking towers, banners, and three visible flags atop the towers.
description_view_1: Rear angled view highlighting the tall central tower, the back wall, and the absence of banners or entrance features.
description_view_2: Side-rear view displaying the arrangement of towers, battlements, and multiple flags, with a clear view of the arrow slits.
description_view_3: Side view focusing on the rear towers and walls, with visible battlements and a partial view of the banners on the side.
